message:
fix(core): recover from poisoned RwLock/Mutex instead of panicking

Replace .expect("lock poisoned") with .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
across backpressure.rs and survival.rs (~15 instances).

When a thread panics while holding a lock, the lock becomes "poisoned" in
Rust's std library. The previous behavior would cascade the panic to any
thread that subsequently tried to acquire the lock, turning one failure
into a process-wide crash. Now we gracefully recover the inner data,
which is safe because the data itself is typically still in a consistent
state — the poisoning is a conservative signal, not proof of corruption.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
